body:
Hi,

My leave-by reminder for clinic bag check stopped firing this week. It's set for 6:15 a.m. on Tuesdays and Thursdays — right before I leave for Willow Creek. Nothing came through either day, and I didn't catch it until I was already pulling out of the driveway.

I checked notification permissions on my end. Everything looks enabled. My husband Neil mentioned the app pushed an update overnight Tuesday, so that's probably it.

I need this one working. Last time I missed a bag check I got to clinic without my fetoscope. Not doing that again. Can you confirm if there's a known issue and what the fix is?

— Brian Santos

Hi Brian,

Thanks for writing in. You tracked it correctly — Tuesday's update caused a notification outage for a subset of users with recurring reminders. The backend issue is resolved, but reminders created before the update sometimes don't register on the new build automatically.

Fix is straightforward: go to Settings > Reminders, tap your clinic bag check entry, delete it, then recreate it with the same 6:15 a.m. schedule. You should get a test ping within two or three minutes of saving. That confirms it's active.

If it still doesn't fire on your next clinic day, reply here with your account email and I'll escalate it directly to engineering.

— Owen
Support Team | Willow App

Owen,

Quick recap since we spoke — today was a full clinic day and the scheduling portal went down around 10 a.m. I had four patients backed up before Dani and I figured out the workaround. Not ideal when you're doing prenatal visits and someone's already anxious before they even hit the waiting room.

I need the incident logged and I need to know what caused it. If this is a recurring failure point, we need a real mitigation plan. Willow Creek doesn't have the staffing buffer for a two-hour scramble.

Brian Santos, CNM
Willow Creek Midwifery

Hi Brian,

Thank you for the follow-up and I'm sorry the outage hit you mid-clinic. I pulled the incident report — it was tied to a server migration that ran approximately 90 minutes past its maintenance window. It should not have overlapped with active clinic hours, and I've already escalated that scheduling failure to our infrastructure team.

I've opened a formal incident ticket (#4821) on your account. You'll receive a written root cause summary within 48 hours. I'm also attaching our offline scheduling workaround guide so your team has it ready going forward.

Let me know if you need anything else from my end.

Owen Santos
Customer Support

Owen,

Ticket number noted. The workaround guide is useful — I'll hand it to Dani since she's the one who actually kept us on track today.

48 hours works. If the root cause summary flags anything that requires a change on our end, say so plainly. I'd rather adjust our setup now than find out the hard way on the next clinic day.

Thanks for the quick turnaround.

Brian

Owen,

Followed up on the ticket I opened last week — the leave-by reminder for my morning clinic bag check still isn't landing on time. It's set for 6:45 AM and I need it to actually fire at 6:45 AM, not somewhere around 7:02 when I'm already backing out of the driveway. I run a full bag check before every clinic day at Willow Creek and if I skip it because the reminder missed me, I'm the one scrambling mid-shift.

Please let me know what's causing the delay and how to fix it.

— Brian Santos

Hi Brian,

Thanks for the follow-up. I pulled your account and found the likely culprit — there's a known delivery lag on devices with background app refresh restricted or battery optimization set to aggressive. The reminder is queuing correctly on our end but getting held up before it surfaces.

Can you check whether Background App Refresh is enabled for the app in your phone settings? That resolves it in most cases. If it's already on, reply here and I'll trigger a manual resync from our side and dig deeper.

Sorry for the hassle — we'll get it sorted.

— Owen | Willow Support Team

Owen,

Background refresh is on. Checked this morning. Reminder still came in late — I'd already started the bag check from memory by the time it showed up. I can do it from memory, but that's not the point of having a reminder system.

For context: Hazel had a full meltdown at 6:20 over her shoes, so my buffer time was already gone. I need this thing to be reliable on clinic days especially. Please do the manual resync and let me know if there's anything I should change on my end.

— Brian

Hi Brian,

Manual resync is done. I also found a secondary issue — your account had a stale device token from an older phone that was creating a delivery conflict. Cleared it out, and your current device is now the only active endpoint.

I moved your reminder trigger time to 6:40 AM so you have a real buffer before your 6:45 target. If it misfires tomorrow morning, reply here and it routes directly back to me — no need to reopen a new ticket.

Hope the clinic day goes smoothly. (And solidarity on the 6:20 shoe situation.)

— Owen | Willow Support Team

Hi Owen,

Following up on the ticket I submitted this morning. The scheduling portal went down right around 8 AM — worst possible timing since we had a full clinic day and a staff meeting stacked after. Dani had to pull up paper intake forms we haven't touched since 2022. Not ideal.

The system came back up around noon but two afternoon appointments didn't sync correctly. Mrs. Calloway at 2:15 and the 3:30 new intake are both showing as unconfirmed. Can you confirm whether automated reminders went out or if we need to call those patients manually?

We're at Willow Creek Midwifery in Asheville — ticket #4471 if that helps you pull the account faster.

Brian Santos, CNM

Hi Brian,

Thank you for the details — ticket #4471 pulled right up and the account notes made this easy to trace.

I can confirm the outage affected a subset of accounts between 7:52 and 11:34 AM. The sync gap is real: neither the 2:15 nor the 3:30 appointment received automated reminders. I've manually triggered confirmation messages to both patients now. They should receive a text or email within the next 15 minutes depending on their notification preferences on file.

I've also applied a service credit to your account for the disruption on what was clearly a high-volume day.

Let me know if anything else looks off once the afternoon sync finishes — happy to escalate to our technical team if data is still mismatched.

Owen Santos
Customer Support, ClinicFlow

Owen,

Following up on the Gmail API ticket from last week. Still hitting a 403 error when the clinic integration tries to pull appointment confirmation emails. Dani tried re-authenticating on her end too. Same result.

We need this fixed before Thursday. That's a clinic day and the automated reminder pull is the only thing keeping our no-show rate reasonable. Without it someone is on the phone for an hour.

Last update said the OAuth scope change was pending review. Can you confirm where that stands?

— Brian
Willow Creek Midwifery, Asheville

Hi Brian,

Good news — the OAuth scope update was approved and deployed this morning. That should clear the 403. To get it working, Dani will need to revoke the existing token and go through the consent screen again. Full re-authorization, not just a refresh. Takes about two minutes.

If the error persists after that, reply here with the exact error string and I'll escalate to Tier 2 right away.

Also — and I cannot believe I'm doing this over a support ticket — tell Mom hi from me.

Owen Santos
Support Tier 1 | TechDesk Help Center

Owen,

Dani re-authorized. Reminders are pulling through. We're good.

You have my cell number. You could have texted. We share a refrigerator. Mostly, when you actually come home.

Thanks for the fast turnaround. Closing this out.

— Brian

Hi Owen,

Following up on the ticket I submitted last week about the Google Calendar API integration. The sync between our clinic scheduling tool and my personal calendar stopped working — again. I'm missing leave-by reminders for clinic bag check on Tuesdays and Thursdays, which means I'm leaving the house without half my kit. Dani covered for me Wednesday. That's not a long-term solution.

The error I'm getting is a 403 when the app tries to refresh the token. I've already re-authenticated twice. Didn't help.

Let me know what you need from my end to dig into this.

Brian

Hi Brian,

Thanks for following up — sorry this is still giving you trouble. A 403 on token refresh usually points to one of two things: the OAuth scope was downgraded at some point, or the service account lost calendar access permissions on the Google Workspace side.

Can you confirm a couple of things for me? First, which Google account is tied to the integration — your personal Gmail or a Willow Creek domain account? Second, do you have admin access to the Google Workspace console, or would that go through someone else at the clinic?

Once I know that I can walk you through exactly what to check. Shouldn't take long to sort out.

Owen
Support Team

Owen,

It's the Willow Creek domain account — brian.santos@gmail.com. I do not have admin access. That would be our office manager, Paula. She's in Tuesday mornings only, which is also my clinic day, so coordination has been fun. (It hasn't been fun.)

I've cc'd Paula on this email. She's responsive by email even when she's not in the office. If you need her to change a permission or flip a setting on the Workspace side, she's your person.

Also — Neil asked if this could be affecting the shared family calendar too. I told him probably not but I honestly don't know. Is that a separate issue or connected?

Brian

Hi Brian,

Great, that helps a lot. I can see the issue now — the calendar.events scope was removed from the OAuth client during a routine permission audit on the Workspace side. That's what's causing the 403. Paula just needs to re-authorize the app under admin settings and re-enable that scope. I've written up the exact steps and sent them to her directly so you don't have to play telephone.

On the family calendar question: if it's under a personal Google account and not the Willow Creek domain, it's a separate setup and shouldn't be affected. Neil's access would be fine.

Once Paula makes the change, give the integration about 15 minutes to re-authenticate and test a sync. Your leave-by reminders should start populating again after that. Let me know if anything looks off.

Owen
Support Team

Hi Owen,

Following up on the Twilio API issue I reported last week. Appointment reminder messages still aren't going out. We had three patients show up late on Tuesday's clinic day because they got no confirmation text. That's a fixable problem and I'd like it fixed.

Dani is seeing the same failure on her end — webhook isn't returning a 200. I don't know if it's a credentials mismatch or something on your side, but I need the logs from Tuesday morning, roughly 8 to 11 a.m., to figure out where it's breaking. Can you pull those?

Thursday is a full clinic day plus staff meeting. I can't be running down missed appointments on top of that. If there's a patch or a config change needed, tell me what it is and I'll get it done tonight after the kids are down.

— Brian Santos, CNM
Willow Creek Midwifery, Asheville

Hi Brian,

Thanks for the detail — it pointed me straight to the problem. I pulled the Tuesday logs and found it: your Twilio auth token rotated automatically on the 22nd and the webhook configuration wasn't updated to match the new credentials. Every outbound request has been failing auth since then.

I've updated the token on the account level. I'm also sending a separate link to your registered email so you can sync the new credentials in your dashboard — it's about a four-step process, should take under five minutes. Once that's done, fire off a single test message before Thursday to confirm delivery.

If Dani is still hitting errors after the sync, have her open a ticket directly and reference yours — I'll link them and handle it the same day.

Let me know when the test goes through.

Owen Santos
Twilio Customer Support

Hi Owen,

Following up on the ticket I submitted last week about our Airtable API integration. We use Airtable to manage the prenatal appointment flow here at Willow Creek, and sometime Tuesday our read requests started throwing 422 errors. Dani caught it first — she was pulling up a client record during our staff meeting and nothing would load.

I've already checked the API key. It's active, scope looks correct, base ID hasn't changed. Error log is attached.

Cluster of clinic days coming up Thursday. If this isn't resolved by 8 a.m. I'm logging appointments on paper, which I would very much like to not do.

— Brian Santos, CNM
Willow Creek Midwifery, Asheville

Hi Brian,

Thanks for the log — that made this a lot faster to track down. The 422s are coming from a field type mismatch. A recent API update started enforcing stricter validation on linked record fields, and your integration script is passing a plain string value where it now expects an array.

The fix is small: wrap the record ID in an array — ["recXXXXXXXX"] instead of "recXXXXXXXX". If you can share the relevant section of your script I can point to the exact lines. Otherwise the pattern should be consistent across any PATCH call hitting a linked record field.

Let me know once you've tested and whether Thursday clinic is back on track.

Owen Santos
Airtable Customer Support

Owen,

Array fix worked. Dani ran a test pull this morning — prenatal flow base is loading clean. Thursday is saved. Hazel woke up at 5 a.m. so I was already awake to see the confirmation come through, which, fine, the universe provides.

One follow-up: we have a second base for postpartum check-in tracking that also uses PATCH calls. Should I audit those linked record fields proactively, or does the stricter validation only apply in specific contexts? Would rather find it now than during a clinic day.

Thanks for the fast turnaround.

— Brian

Owen,

Followed up on the ticket I opened last week — the leave-by reminder for my morning clinic bag check still isn't landing on time. It's set for 6:45 AM and I need it to actually fire at 6:45 AM, not somewhere around 7:02 when I'm already backing out of the driveway. I run a full bag check before every clinic day at Willow Creek and if I skip it because the reminder missed me, I'm the one scrambling mid-shift.

Please let me know what's causing the delay and how to fix it.

— Brian Santos

Hi Brian,

Thanks for the follow-up. I pulled your account and found the likely culprit — there's a known delivery lag on devices with background app refresh restricted or battery optimization set to aggressive. The reminder is queuing correctly on our end but getting held up before it surfaces.

Can you check whether Background App Refresh is enabled for the app in your phone settings? That resolves it in most cases. If it's already on, reply here and I'll trigger a manual resync from our side and dig deeper.

Sorry for the hassle — we'll get it sorted.

— Owen | Willow Support Team

Owen,

Background refresh is on. Checked this morning. Reminder still came in late — I'd already started the bag check from memory by the time it showed up. I can do it from memory, but that's not the point of having a reminder system.

For context: Hazel had a full meltdown at 6:20 over her shoes, so my buffer time was already gone. I need this thing to be reliable on clinic days especially. Please do the manual resync and let me know if there's anything I should change on my end.

— Brian

Hi Brian,

Manual resync is done. I also found a secondary issue — your account had a stale device token from an older phone that was creating a delivery conflict. Cleared it out, and your current device is now the only active endpoint.

I moved your reminder trigger time to 6:40 AM so you have a real buffer before your 6:45 target. If it misfires tomorrow morning, reply here and it routes directly back to me — no need to reopen a new ticket.

Hope the clinic day goes smoothly. (And solidarity on the 6:20 shoe situation.)

— Owen | Willow Support Team

Owen —

Long clinic day. Ran two hours over and I still have charting to finish before I can get home to Neil and the kids. Before I lose track of it — can you confirm whether the supply reorder I submitted on the 16th actually went through? Dani flagged it at staff meeting and I told her I'd follow up. We're getting low on a few things and I'd rather not improvise.

Also need someone to look at the scheduling block for next Tuesday. There's a gap sitting there that shouldn't be. Can you check what happened?

Brian
Willow Creek Midwifery, Asheville

Hi Brian,

Looked into both.

The supply order from the 16th did process, but there's a delay on the distributor end — revised delivery estimate is Thursday the 2nd. I've escalated it to priority status so it doesn't slip again. Confirmation should hit your clinic inbox within the hour.

On the Tuesday block — looks like an appointment got bumped to Thursday when the system updated last week. I've restored the original slot. Should look correct now.

Let me know if anything else surfaces from today.

Owen Santos
Willow Creek Support

Hi Owen,

Putting in a ticket because my Tuesday and Thursday leave-by reminder for clinic bag check hasn't fired in two weeks. Zero notifications. I've been catching it only because Hazel kicked the bag over in the hallway on her way to the bathroom — not a system I want to depend on.

I need that alert at 6:45 AM. I'm out the door by 7:15 on clinic days and the bag check is the last thing I can do with a functioning brain before everything gets loud. Doppler, documentation binder, backup gloves. Missing any of that is a real problem once I'm at Willow Creek.

Account is brian.santos@gmail.com. Let me know what you need.

— Brian

Hi Brian,

Thanks for writing in. I pulled your account and found the issue — your Tuesday/Thursday reminder was still linked to a device token that expired when you switched phones last month. The schedule was intact, the delivery just had nowhere to go.

I've reset the token and re-linked it to your current device. A test notification should hit within the next few minutes. If it doesn't show, reply here and I'll push it manually from my end.

Also noted your 6:45 AM time and flagged it so the fall DST shift won't move it on you without warning.

— Owen | Willow Support Team

Owen,

Test came through. Good — I was already pulling the bag out to double-check the Doppler for tomorrow anyway.

One thing before I close this out: can you confirm the recurrence is set to weekly and not one-time? Neil will tell you I am not great at noticing when something quietly breaks until it's already been broken for a month. I'd rather not be back here in October.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

— Brian

Owen,

The patient scheduling module went down twice during clinic hours on Thursday — once around 10 a.m. and again just after 2. We had a full slate of appointments and ended up tracking three patients on paper, which is not how I want to run a clinic day.

It came up in staff meeting. Dani Cho flagged it first and the rest of us confirmed we'd seen the same thing. There's now a conversation on the table about switching platforms entirely, which I'd really rather not open.

Can you pull the error logs from Thursday and tell me what actually happened? We need this stable before next week.

— Brian Santos, CNM
Willow Creek Midwifery, Asheville

Hi Brian,

Thanks for reaching out. I pulled Thursday's logs — the root cause was a database timeout triggered by an unoptimized background query that fired at 10:14 a.m. It caused a cascade that took the scheduling module offline twice in the window you described. We've identified and patched the query on our end.

One thing I need to confirm: are you currently on version 4.2.1 of the module? That's where the fix has been deployed. If your instance hasn't updated yet, I can walk your IT contact through the process — it's a quick apply.

Let me know and I'll get an incident report over to you as well.

Owen Santos
Technical Support | MidwiveSoft

Owen,

I have absolutely no idea what version we're on. I'm a midwife. I forwarded your email to Dani — she handles the tech side of things here and will be able to confirm. Expect to hear from her by end of day tomorrow.

Thursday was a lot. Full schedule, staff meeting after, everybody running on about four hours of sleep collectively. (Hazel was up half the night before so that's just my household's contribution to the chaos.) If it crashes again next clinic day I cannot promise a calm support ticket.

Dani's on it. Thank you for the quick turnaround.

— Brian

Hi Brian,

Perfect — I'll watch for Dani's message. Once I confirm the version, the update itself takes under ten minutes to apply and won't interrupt any open sessions.

I'm also flagging your account for priority monitoring through the next two clinic days. If anything unusual surfaces in the logs, we'll catch it before it becomes a problem at your end.

Incident report is already drafted — I'll send it over once the version check is done. Good to have on file, especially since it came up formally in the staff meeting.

Talk soon.

Owen Santos
Technical Support | MidwiveSoft
